pi_items.source: Task description specifies X Euler (index 0) and Z Euler (index 2); export_result.sh and verifier.py both check these indices

Task description recommends 0.01-0.1 as 'realistic'; Blender Artists forum discusses ~0.1 as typical for camera shake. Metadata allows 0.005-0.2 which is broader than task description's recommended range.
Task description recommends 0.01-0.1 as 'realistic'; Blender Artists forum discusses ~0.1 as typical. Metadata allows 0.005-0.2 which is broader.
Task description recommends 5-50 as 'realistic'; Blender Artists forum discussion mentions 10-20 as practical values. Metadata allows 2.0-100.0 which is broader.
Task description recommends 5-50 as 'realistic'; Blender Artists forum discussion mentions 10-20 as practical values. Metadata allows 2.0-100.0 which is much broader.
setup_blender.sh lines 64-76 downloads BMW27.blend.zip from official Blender demo repository
HTTP HEAD request to official URL returns Content-Length: 3009385; setup_blender.sh line 67 documents expected size as '3MB'
setup_blender.sh creates configuration directories for Blender 4.2 (lines 19-26)

privileged_info_summary: The handheld_camera_shake task uses the BMW27.blend file, a real official Blender demonstration scene (3MB, from https://download.blender.org/demo/test/BMW27.blend.zip). The task requires adding Noise F-Curve modifiers to the Camera object's X (rotation_euler.0) and Z (rotation_euler.2) rotation channels, with the output saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/handheld_cam.blend. The task description specifies realistic parameter ranges as Scale 5-50 and Strength 0.01-0.1, while the verifier allows broader ranges (Scale 2-100, Strength 0.005-0.2).
